2603.24967.title: The Anatomy of Uncertainty in LLMs
2603.24967.paper_summary: Understanding why a large language model (LLM) is uncertain about the response is important for their reliable deployment. Current approaches, which either provide a single uncertainty score or rely on the classical aleatoric-epistemic dichotomy, fail to offer actionable insights for improving the generative model. Recent studies have also shown that such methods are not enough for understanding uncertainty in LLMs. In this work, we advocate for an uncertainty decomposition framework that dissects LLM uncertainty into three distinct semantic components: (i) input ambiguity, arising from ambiguous prompts; (ii) knowledge gaps, caused by insufficient parametric evidence; and (iii) decoding randomness, stemming from stochastic sampling. Through a series of experiments we demonstrate that the dominance of these components can shift across model size and task. Our framework provides a better understanding to audit LLM reliability and detect hallucinations, paving the way for targeted interventions and more trustworthy systems.
2603.24967.paper_summary_zh: 理解為什麼大型語言模型 (LLM) 對回應感到不確定對於其可靠部署非常重要。<lb/>當前的方法，要么提供單一的不確定性分數，要么依賴於傳統的隨機性-認識性二分法，未能提供可行的見解來改善生成模型。<lb/>最近的研究也顯示，這些方法不足以理解 LLM 中的不確定性。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提倡一種不確定性分解框架，將 LLM 的不確定性分解為三個不同的語義組件：<lb/>(i) 輸入模糊性，源於模糊的提示；<lb/>(ii) 知識缺口，由於參數證據不足而引起；<lb/>(iii) 解碼隨機性，源於隨機抽樣。<lb/>通過一系列實驗，我們展示了這些組件的主導地位可以隨著模型大小和任務而變化。<lb/>我們的框架提供了更好的理解，以審核 LLM 的可靠性並檢測幻覺，為有針對性的干預和更可靠的系統鋪平道路。
2603.24967.author: Aditya Taparia et.al.
2603.24967.authors: Aditya Taparia, Ransalu Senanayake, Kowshik Thopalli, Vivek Narayanaswamy
